summary:
- Claim: Vitamin C increases non-heme iron absorption through reduction of Fe³⁺ to Fe²⁺.  
- Evidence: Iron absorption improves significantly when consumed alongside vitamin C-rich foods; this effect has been consistently demonstrated in human studies.  
- Answer: B

- Answer: B. Answer choice: B
